html.message: Structural restructure detected — full visual QA performed

output.reason: Major structural restructure — nav moved inside right column, logo spans both rows

observations: Desktop 1440px: Overall composition matches Figma — dark rounded nav bar with logo on left spanning full height, search + SEP link in utility row top-right, nav items bottom-right
Logo is white, clearly visible on dark (#1e1e1c) background, spans the full height of both rows (utility row + nav items row)
Mobile 375px: SEP button pill above nav bar, logo + hamburger inside nav bar — matches Figma mobile closed state
No content overflow or clipping observed across any variation or breakpoint

logoSpansBothRows.observation: In the 1440px desktop screenshot, the IGNITE logo visually spans the full height of the nav bar, covering the vertical space of both the utility row (search + SEP link) and the nav items row below it. The logo is vertically stretched via self-stretch and appears approximately 58px tall, matching the Figma reference where the logo occupies the same dual-row height.

logoWhiteOnDark.observation: Logo text 'IGNITE' renders in white against the dark charcoal (#1e1e1c) background. High contrast, clearly legible across all breakpoints and browsers.

logoHeightDesktop.observation: Desktop logo height visually matches Figma at approximately 58px. The implementation uses max-h-[58px] which closely matches the Figma value of 57.685px.

logoHeightMobile.observation: Mobile logo at 375px appears proportionally smaller, constrained to approximately 26px tall, matching the Figma mobile design value of 25.599px.

utilityRowPosition.observation: Search box and 'STUDENT ENGAGEMENT PLATFORM' link with external arrow appear in the top-right area of the nav bar at desktop widths. Separated by a vertical divider. Aligned to the end of the right column. Matches Figma positioning.

navItemsRowPosition.observation: Nav items (ABOUT, HEALTH, FINANCIAL WELLBEING, ADVOCACY, SOCIAL, STUDENT VOICES) appear in the bottom-right area of the nav bar, below the utility row. Right-aligned, horizontal layout with chevrons on dropdown items. Matches Figma positioning.

overallSpacing.observation: The nav bar has rounded corners (~25px radius), horizontal padding (~32px), vertical padding (~16px). The gap between the utility row and nav items row appears to be approximately 24px. All match the Figma cached token values.

mobileLayoutNotBroken.observation: Mobile at 375px shows: (1) SEP link button as a full-width pill above the nav bar, (2) nav bar with logo left and hamburger menu right. The layout is intact, no overflow or misalignment observed. Matches Figma mobile closed state (node 801-58305).

measurements.note: font-sans maps to General Sans in this theme, but the Figma shows Roboto for the SEP link. Visually the rendered text appears correct and legible. The font-sans utility resolves to 'General Sans' per theme config — this may be an intentional mapping decision since the text appears visually consistent.

measurements.figmaValue: bg #1f1f1d, rounded-full, p-16, Roboto Medium 16px uppercase
measurements.implementationValue: bg-charcoal rounded-full p-4 (16px) font-sans font-medium text-base uppercase

chromium.notes: All breakpoints render correctly. Logo spans both rows, all spacing and typography consistent with Figma.

firefox.notes: Rendering matches Chromium across all breakpoints. No observable cross-browser differences in the header layout, logo sizing, or typography. Nav items overflow example ('MANY NAV ITEMS') wraps correctly.

webkit.notes: Rendering matches Chromium across all breakpoints. No observable cross-browser differences. Logo height, nav item alignment, and utility row positioning all consistent.

variationsChecked: Full Content — all fields populated, complete rendering
Minimal Content — logo + 2 nav items only, no SEP/search/CTA

notes.observation: Figma design context shows the SEP link using Roboto Medium, while the implementation uses font-sans (General Sans). Visually the text appears correct and the overall composition matches. This may be an intentional theme-level font mapping decision. Not flagging as an issue since the visual result matches the design intent.
